name: The oracle against Edom
notes: Obadiah's vision against Edom and Jeremiah's oracle against Edom carry the same word from the LORD, the shared phrasing running so close as to be near-verbatim. Both open with the rumour heard from the LORD and the ambassador sent among the heathen, both make Edom small and despised, both rebuke the pride of the heart that dwells in the clefts of the rock and exalts its nest like the eagle, and both promise that the LORD will bring it down. The figures of the grapegatherers and the thieves who leave a remnant, the searching out of Esau's hidden things, the perishing of wisdom from Teman, and the dismay of the mighty men all recur in each. The two prophets are given one word against Edom, and the shared language shows the one Spirit speaking through both. The differing arrangement and the detail each keeps in its own place are left as a matter for further study.

links.label: a rumour from the LORD and an ambassador is sent ... the heathen
I have made thee small among the heathen ... despised
The pride of thine heart hath deceived thee thou that dwellest in the clefts of the rock
thy nest ... as the eagle ... I will bring thee down
if the grapegatherers came to thee would they not leave some grapes
How are his hidden things sought up ... I have uncovered his secret places
destroy the wise men out of Edom ... Is wisdom no more in Teman
thy mighty men ... shall be dismayed
